steps.title: Spot the comma splice
steps.content: After “Laura A. Burkle,” there's a comma, and then “the study examined” is an independent clause. This creates a comma splice—two independent clauses improperly joined by a comma.

steps.title: Fix the boundary correctly
steps.content:
Option D uses a period after “United States” to separate the two independent clauses, fixing the comma splice. The comma after the introductory phrase “Led by the researcher Laura A. Burkle” is then correct, as introductory phrases require a comma before the main clause. Options A, B, and C maintain the comma splice by keeping a comma before “the study examined.”

That matches choice D — D is correct.
